idea.title: Agent Dispatcher
idea.hook: Route incoming tasks to cheap or expensive AI agents — wrong routing burns your token budget before the queue clears.
idea.prototype: Two-column board. LEFT column shows incoming task cards with hidden difficulty 1-5 (telegraphed via size/color hints). RIGHT column shows 4 agent slots: Haiku (1 tok, succeeds at diff ≤2), Sonnet (3 tok, ≤4), Opus (8 tok, all), Human (0 tok but 3 turns busy). Drag a task onto an agent. Cheap agent on too-hard task = task doubles. 50 tokens to clear 20 tasks.
idea.mutation_of: Token Budget (32_token_budget) — sibling spend-management mechanic, but here the spend is per-routing-decision rather than per-action-chain, and the agent-fit mini-puzzle adds a hidden-info layer.
idea.why_novel: Combines spend-budget pressure with agent-fit puzzle reading; failures double tasks (positive feedback on mistakes) instead of just losing money. Topical AI-economics framing — players actually face the trade-off Anthropic API users make every day.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Token spend budget (INITIAL_BUDGET=50) + growing queue length on routing failures
idea.lose_condition: Tokens reach 0 with at least one task still in the queue

sources.title: AI agent cost optimisation
sources.snippet: Devs are increasingly trade-off-aware about which agent handles which task — cheap models for cheap calls, expensive ones reserved for hard cases.

sources.title: Routing as a skill
sources.snippet: Routing decisions in multi-agent stacks are where most of the budget actually leaks — mis-classifying a hard task as easy is the dominant failure mode.

synthesizer_brief: Take the AI-cost-optimisation framing from timurtaepov/1086-1087 and turn it into a hidden-difficulty routing puzzle. Player is the dispatcher, agents are LLM tiers (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus + Human fallback), pressure is a tight token budget. Mis-routing penalises with a task-doubling rebound rather than a death — keeps the round alive while compounding the budget pressure.
